\'02; -720 \'03(\'05); \'02; -720 \'01\'06; \'01; -360 \'01\'07; \'01; -144 \'01\'08; \'01; -144 ; \* \* Pr Font34 Bin0 BinSub0 Frac0 Def1 Margin0 Margin0 Jc1 Indent1440 Lim0 Lim1 Friend of the Court Investigation Expenses Model LAO William J Bartels MarksD \* MSC \* http://schemas.microsoft.com/office/word/2003/wordml \* 013f \* \* \* -20 \* \* \* .\* .\* .\* )\* ( )\* ( ) \* ( )\* ( )\* ( ) State Court Administrative Office Model Local Administrative Order 34 - \* Access_to_Friend_of_the_Court_Records \* Access_to_Friend_of_the_Court_RecordsFriend of the Court Investigation Expenses (rev. 10 /10 ) [LOCAL COURT LETTERHEAD] Administrative Order [year] - [number] Rescinds previous Administrative Order [year] - [number] FRIEND OF THE COURT INVESTIGATION EXPENSES [Optional] The court finds that [include f indings on average costs of conducting an investigation and making a report , or other facts that support the charges being implemented]. IT IS ORDERED: This administrative order is issued in accordance with MCL 552.505(3) and SCAO Administrative Memorandum 2010- 05 Standards for Charging Friend of the Court Investigation Expenses , effective [insert effective date] . The purpose of this order is to implement charg es pursuant to MCL\~552.505(3) upon approval by the State Court Administrative Office. 1. -720 Definitions \'93 Expense \'94 means any direct or indirect cost for an investigation or submitting a report . \'93 Investigation \'94 means the work done on a case to organize or gather information needed to fulfill the office s duties under MCL 552.505 (1) (g) . \'93 Office \'94 means the [County] Friend of the Court. \'93 Report \'94 means the work and expenses associated with preparing and submitting a written document regarding an investigation to the court and parties . \'93 Request \'94 means an express statement by a party or party s attorney asking for an investigation , whether made in a document submitted to the office or court, a statement made on the record, or an order stipulating to an investigation, or a court finding that a party requested an investigation. 2. -720 Charges for Expenses a. -360 The office shall maintain sufficient records to document the basis of its determination that a party has requested an investigation, and to determine whether the amount charged exceeds the office s expenses for that case . b. If a party requests an investigation, t he office shall assess charge s [select one] for expenses attributable to an investigation . [explain how charges are calculated or include a chart that specifies rates and charges ] The office shall not charge for its expenses when it is notified that the court has waived or suspended fees because of inability to pay or indigence. for its expenses according to the following schedule : Width3 Width7560 WidthB3 Width3 Width5220 Width3 Width2340 -25 Expense: Amount Width3 Width7560 WidthB3 Width3 Width5220 Width3 Width2340 Width3 Width7560 WidthB3 WidthA3 WidthA83 Width3 Width5220 Width3 Width2257 -25 [Full ] Custody Investigation [and Report] Width3 Width7560 WidthB3 WidthA3 WidthA83 Width3 Width5220 Width3 Width2257 -25 [Full ] Parenting Time Investigation [and Report] Width3 Width7560 WidthB3 WidthA3 WidthA83 Width3 Width5220 Width3 Width2257 -25 [Limited Investigation ][ and Report] Width3 Width7560 WidthB3 WidthA3 WidthA83 Width3 Width5220 Width3 Width2257 -25 [Issuing a Report] Width3 Width7560 WidthB3 WidthA3 WidthA83 Width3 Width5220 Width3 Width2257 -25 [ List Other Expenses ] Width3 Width7560 WidthB3 WidthA3 WidthA83 Width3 Width5220 Width3 Width2257 -25 Investigation and Report - Court Waives or Suspends Fees Due to Inability to Pay or Indigence $0.00 Width3 Width7560 WidthB3 WidthA3 WidthA83 Width3 Width5220 Width3 Width2257 -25 [Investigation and Report Reduced Rate for [explai n] [ Court Partially Waives or Suspends Fees, Limited Ability to Pay, etc. ] ] Width3 Width7560 WidthB3 WidthA3 WidthA83 Width3 Width5220 Width3 Width2257 -25 [Actual cost for: [ list ] \'93Actual Cost \'94 ] Width3 Width7560 WidthB3 WidthA3 WidthA83 Width3 Width5220 Width3 Width2257 c. -360 Unless the court orders a party to pay an amount prior to the office starting an investigation , the office shall bill [specify who ] [ plaintiff, defendant , petitioner , party requesting investigation , both parties (and specify allocation) , etc.] for its expenses [specify when ] [immediately upon starting an investigation , upon submission of a report to the court and parties , or other specified point in process , etc. ] , or any time within [insert number] days following entry of an order that resolves the disputed matter if the office discovers that a party requested an investigation . Any bill provided pursuant to this order shall contain instructions on submitting a payment and specify when payment is due . [ If payable through the clerk or if the court wants for its records : The office shall submit a copy of the bill to the clerk of the court . ] d. -360 Unless a party disput es the amount charged on a case, the office shall presume that its charges do not exceed its actual expenses. If a party, who has paid the billed amount , requests confirmation that the charges exceed the office s actual expenses on a case , the office shall provide an itemized statement showing actual expense s . If the amount collected exceeds the actual expenses that the office can document for a case, the office shall refund the difference. [optional][If a party, who has failed to pay the billed amount , disputes the charges , the office shall prepare an amended bill that itemizes the office s actual expenses and credits any amounts paid on that case.] 3. -720 Payments and Refunds a. -360 The [select one] [friend of the court office] [clerk of the court] shall receive and process payments for amounts charged pursuant to MCL 552.505(3) . b. To be considered timely, a party must pay as ordered in a case or within [insert number] days of the office providing a bill for its expense s . c. The office shall return any amount collected that exceeds its total expenses. d. The office shall notify the court if a party fails to pay the amount charged [insert instances when the court wants notice that a party failed to pay] by [insert terms under which the court wants to recei ve notification from the office] , so the court can take additional action in that case . Effective Date: Date: Chief Judge Signature: INSTRUCTIONS for Model 34 The SCAO has developed this model local administrative order to govern local internal processes implementing MCL 552.505(3). For additional information regarding c harg es for friend of the court investigation expenses, please refer to SCAO A dministrative Memorandum 2010- 05 . The c ourt may only collect amounts for conducting investigations as authorized by this LAO. The model order and A dministrative Memorandum 2010- 05 provide the necessary parameters that the local FOC office need s to follow to begin collecting amounts to defray the costs of conducting an investigation . A c ourt collecting any amount to defray the costs of custody or parenting time investigations must use this LAO, and should customize it for their circuit and submit it for approval pursuant to MCR 8.112. If you have any questions or are in need of further information, please contact your regional administrator or Management Analyst William J. \'01; -360 \'02\'04.; \'01; -360 \'02\'05.; \'01; -180 \'02\'06.; \'01; -360 \'02\'07.; \'01; -360 \'02\'08.; \'01; -180 ; \* \* Pr Font34 Bin0 BinSub0 Frac0 Def1 Margin0 Margin0 Jc1 Indent1440 Lim0 Lim1 Trisha Cuellar Deborah Marks \* Michigan Supreme Court \* \* http://schemas.microsoft.com/office/word/2003/wordml \* 2450 \* \* \* \* \* PAGE \\* MERGEFORMAT 8 \* .\* .\* .\* ) \* ( )\* ( )\* ( )\* ( )\* ( ) -240 \* SEQ CHAPTER \ \ 1 State Court Administrative Office -240 Model Local Administrative Order 48 Friend of the Court Alternative Dispute Resolution\* insert_number_-_insert_title\* insert_number_-_insert_title (rev. 09/19) -240 [LOCAL COURT LETTERHEAD] -240 Administrative Order [year] - [number] FRIEND OF THE COURT ALTERNATIVE DISPUTE RESOLUTION Previous Friend of the Court Alternative Dispute Resolution Local Administrative Orders Rescinded by this Order: IT IS ORDERED: P ursuant to MCR 3.224, the following is adopted at the [insert local court number] as the Friend of the Court Alternative Dispute Resolution Plan: (A) -540 Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR) General Provisions The [insert local court number] has determined that Friend of the Court (FOC) ADR can assist parties and the court in resolving cus \* FILLIN X \\* MERGEFORMAT tody, parenting time, and support disputes . 1) -720 The [insert local court number] will use the following ADR practices: -630 ( X ) FOC domestic relations mediation as established in MCL 552.513 and MCR 3.224, ( ) f acil itative and information-gathering c onference as established in MCR 3.224, ( ) joint m eetings established in MCL 552.642a and MCR 3.224, ( ) t he [insert local court number] has establish ed the following additional FOC ADR processes [explain name and legal authority] . -720 2) -720 All FOC cases must be screened for domestic violence using SCAO s screening protocol before the ADR process begins. If domestic violence is identified or suspected, the ADR process may not continue unless the protected party submits a wri tten consent and the FOC takes additional precautions to ensure the safety of the protected party and court staff. Throughout the ADR process, the mediator or facilitator must make reasonable efforts to screen for the presence of coerci on or violence that would make ADR physically or emotionally unsafe for any participant or that would impede achieving a voluntary and safe resolution of issues. -720 3) -720 I n accordance with MCL 552.505a , the FOC may provide ADR services for all open FOC cases that qualify for FOC ADR services . -720 4) -720 A party may object to FOC ADR under MCR 3.224(E) . An objection must be based on one or more of the factors listed in MCR 3. 224 (D)(2) : -720 -360 (a) child abuse or neglect; (b) domestic abuse, unless the protected party submits a written consent and the friend of the court takes additional precautions to ensure the safety of the protected party and court staff; (c) inability of one or both parties to negotiate for themselves at the ADR, unless attorneys for both parties will be present at the ADR session; (d) reason to believe that one or both parties health or safety would be endangered by ADR; or (e) for other good cause shown. -720 -720 and must allege facts in support of the objection. Timely objections must be made in accordance with MCR 3.224(E). 5) -720 Parties who are, or have been, subject to a personal protection order or other protective order or who are involved in a past or present chil d abuse and neglect proceeding may not be referred to FOC ADR without a hearing to determine whether FOC ADR is appropriate. The court may order ADR if a protected party requests it without holding a hearing. -720 6) -720 The FOC may exempt cases from ADR based on MCR 3.224(D)(2) . The FOC shall notify the court when it exempt s a case from FOC ADR. If the FOC exempts a case from ADR, a party may file a motion and schedule a hearing to request the court to order FOC ADR. -720 7) -720 Attorneys of record will be allowed to attend, and participate in, all FOC ADR processes, or elect not to attend upon mutual agreement with opposing counsel and their client. -720 8) -720 Participants in an ADR process may not record the ADR proceeding. -720 9) -720 The FOC shall provide a report with each FOC ADR proposed consent order containing sufficient information to allow the court to make an independent determination that the proposed order is in the child s best interest. -720 10) -720 When the parties do not resolve some or all of the issues in a facil itative and information-gathering conference or when the FOC submits a proposed order following a joint meeting, the FOC shall submit a report containing the parties agreed-upon and disputed facts and issues. -720 11) -720 Qualifications: FOC ADR providers ( ) have met the training and qualifications established by SCAO and approved by the c hief j udge ( ) and ( ) have been approved subject to conditions established by SCAO. -720 12) -720 Public Access to FOC ADR Plan: The FOC will ma ke the FOC ADR plan available : ( ) On the court s website -720 ( ) By way of pamphlet, handbook, or other publication ( ) Other (specify) _________________ (B) -540 Submitting Cases to ADR -720 ( ) On written stipulation of the parties, on motion of a party, or on the court s initiative, the court may order any contested ( ) prejudgment ( ) and ( ) postjudgment custody, parenting time, or support issue in a domestic relations case, including postjudgment matters to FOC mediation by written order. -720 -720 ( ) All ( ) prejudgment ( ) postjudgment custody, parenting time, and support issues are ordered to a facilitative and information-gathering conference. ( ) On written stipulation of the parties, on motion of a party, or on the court s own initiative, the court may order the parties to attend a facilitative and information-gathering conference. ( ) On written stipulation of the parties, on motion of a party, on the court s own initiative, the court may order the parties to attend a joint meeting postjudgment . ( ) The FOC may schedule a joint meeting with parties to a postjudgment custody, parenting time, and support dispute. -720 (C) -540 ADR Procedures 1) -720 FOC Domestic Relations Mediation FOC domestic relations mediation is a process in which a neutral third party facilitates confidential communication between parties to explore solutions to settle custody and parenting time or support issues for FOC cases. a. -720 Objection to Mediation: A party who is ordered to FOC domestic relation s mediation may file a written motion to remove the case from FOC med iation and a notice of hearing of the motion, and serve a copy on all parties or their attorneys of record within 14 days after receiving notice of the order. The motion must be set for hearing within 14 days after it is filed, unless the hearin g is adjourned by agreement of counsel or the court orders otherwise. A timely objection will be heard before the case is mediated by the FOC . -720 b. -720 FOC Domestic Relations Mediation Procedures: FOC domestic relations m ediation will be conducted by a mediator selected by the FOC. -720 i. -720 At the beginning of the mediation, the mediator wil l advise the parties and their attorneys, if applicable, of the following: a) -720 The purpose of mediation; b) -720 How the mediator will conduct mediation; c) Except as provided for in MCR 2.412(D)(8), statements made during the mediation process are confidential and cannot be used in court proceedings and cannot be recorded . -720 ii. -720 If the parties reach an agreement, the mediato r shall submit a proposed order and a report pursuant to MCR 3.224(I) within seven days. -720 iii. -720 If the parties do not reach an agreement within seven days of the completion of mediation, the mediator shall so advise the court stating only the date of completion of the process, who participated in the mediation, whether settlement was reached, and whether additional FOC ADR proceedings are contemplated. -720 iv. -720 With the exceptions provided for in MCR 2.412(D), communications during FOC domestic relations mediation process are confidential and cannot be used in court proceedings and cannot be recorded. 2) -360 ( ) Facilitative and Information-Gathering Conference [ Delete if not applicable, and delete this note. ] -720 A n FOC facilitative and information-gathering c onference is a process in which a facilitator assists the pa rties in reaching an agreement regarding custody, parenting time, and support . If the parties fail to reach an agreement, the facilitator may prepare a report and/or recommended order. a. -720 Object ions to a Facilitative and Information-Gathering Conference: -720 i. -720 To object to a FOC facilitative and information-gathering c onference, a party must include the objection within the pleading or post - judgment motion initiating the action, a responsive pleading or answer, or file the objection within 14 days of the date that th e notice is sent to the party. All objections must be filed with the court. -720 ii. -720 The objecting p arty must schedule the hearing and serve a copy of the objection and notice of hearing on all parties and/or attorneys of record. -720 iii. -720 If a party timely obj ects, the FOC shall not hold a facilitative and information-gathering c onference unless the court orders a conference after motion and hearing or the objecting party withdraws the objection . -720 b. -720 FOC Facilitative and Information-Gathering Conference Procedures: An FOC facilitative and information-gathering c onference shall use the following procedure s : i. -720 At the beginning of the conference, the facilitator will advise the parties and their attorneys, if applicable, of the following: -720 a) -720 T he purpose of the conference and how the facilitator will conduct the conference and submit an order or recommendation to the court under MCR 3.224( F) ; -720 b) -720 H ow information gathered during the conference will be used; -720 c) -720 T hat statements made during the conference are not confidential and can be used in other court proceedings, and shall not be recorded; and -720 d) -720 T hat the parties are expected to provide information as required by MCL 552.603 to the FOC and the consequences of not doing so. -720 ii. -720 If the partie s resolve all contested issues, the facilitator shall submit a report pursuant to MCR 3.224(I) ( ) and shall provide a proposed order to the court setting forth the parties agreements. -720 iii. -720 If the parties do not resolve all contested issues at the conference or the parties agree to resolve all or some contested issues but do not sign the proposed order, the facilitator shall submit a report pursuant to MCR 3.224(I). ( ) The facilitator shall also: -720 ( ) Prepare and forward a recommended order to the co urt within seven days from the date of the conference. The court may enter the recommended order if it approves the order and must serve it on all parties and attorneys of record within seven days from the date the court enters the order. Accompanying the order must be a notice that a party may object to the order by filing a written objection with the court within 21 days after the date of service, and by scheduling a hearing on the objection. If there is a timely objection, the court will hold a hearing within 21 days after the objection is filed. If a party objects, the order remains in effect pending a hearing on a party s objection unless the court orders otherwise. -720 -720 ( ) Prepare and serve a recommended o rder on the parties within seven days from the date of the conference along with a notice that the recommended order will be presented to the court for entry unless a party objects by filing a written objection within 21 days after the date of service, and by scheduling a hearing on th e objection. If neither party files a timely objection, the court may enter the order if it approves. -720 -720 ( ) Submit a recommendation to the court for further action the court might take to help the parties resolve the remaining contested issues in the case, or alert the court there are contested issues that might require the court s immediate attention. -720 ( ) [O nly use if the court chooses one of the first two options above . ] A party may consent to entry of a recommended order by signing a copy of the order at the time of the conference or after receiving the recommended order. A party who consents to entry of the order waives the right to object to the order and must file a motion to set the order aside once it enters. iv. -720 Except for communications made during do mestic violence screening , communications made during a facilitative and information-gathering conference are not confiden tial and may be used in court proceedings. 3) -360 ( ) Joint Meetings [ Delete if not applicable , and delete this note. ] -720 Joint meeting s are a process in which a person discusses proposed solutions with the parties to a custody or parenting time complaint or an objection to an FOC support recommendation. a. -720 Objection to a Joint Meeting: i. -720 To object to a joint meeting, the party must file a written objection with the FOC and provide a copy to all parties and their attorneys of record before the time scheduled for the joint meeting. ii. -720 If a party files an objection, the FOC shall not hold a joint meeting unless the court orders a joint meeting foll owing a hearing on motion of a party or the objecting party withdraws the objection. b. -720 Joint Meeting Procedures: Joint meetings shall be conducted as follows: i. -720 At the beginning of a joint meetin g, the person conducting the meeting shall do the following: a) -720 A dvise the parties that state ments made during the joint meeting are not confidential an d can be used in other court proceedings; -720 b) -720 A dvise the parties that the purpose of the meeting is for the parties to reach an accommodation and how the pe rson will conduct the meeting; and -720 c) -720 Advise the parties that the person may recommend an order to the court to resolve the dispute , an d explain to the parties the in formation provided for in MCR 3.224(H)(1)(d)-(e). ii. -720 At the conclusion of a joint me eting, the person conducting the meeting shall either terminate the meeting without further action or do one of the following within seven days : a) -720 If the parties reach an accommodation, record the accommodation in writing and provide a copy to the parties and attorneys of record. If the accommodation modifies an order, the person must submit a proposed order and a report pursuant to MCR 3.224(I) to the court. If the court approves the order, the court shall enter it; or -720 b) -720 Submit an order to the court stating the person s recommendation for resolving the dispute with a report pursuant to MCR 3.224(I) . The parties may consent by signing the recommended order and waiving the objection period in accordance with MCR 3.224(H)(1)(e)(iii). If the court approves the order, the court shall enter it. -720 iii. -720 If the person conducting the joint meeting submi ts a recommended order to the court, t he FOC must serve the parties and attorneys of record with a copy of the ord er and a notice that provides the following information: a) -720 That the court may enter the recommended order resolving the dispute unless a party objects to the order in writing w ithin 21 days after the notice is sent; -720 b) -720 The objection must be sent to [state where to file the objection] ; c) -720 That a party may waive the 21-day objection period by returning a signed copy of the recommended order; -720 d) -720 I f a party files a written objection within the 21-day limit, the FOC shall set a court hearing before a judge or referee to resolve the dispute. If a party fails to file a written objection within the 21-day limit, the FOC shall submit the proposed order to the court for entry if the court approves it; -720 e) -720 E xcept for communications made during domestic violence screening, communications made during a joint meeting are not confidential and may be used in other court proceedings and can not be recorded. Dated: _______________ Chief judge s ignature: _________________________________ \* \'01; -360 \'02\'04.; \'01; -360 \'02\'05.; \'01; -180 \'02\'06.; \'01; -360 \'02\'07.; \'01; -360 \'02\'08.; \'01; -180 ; \* \* Pr Font34 Bin0 BinSub0 Frac0 Def1 Margin0 Margin0 Jc1 Indent1440 Lim0 Lim1 Trisha Cuellar Deborah Marks \* Michigan Supreme Court \* \* http://schemas.microsoft.com/office/word/2003/wordml \* 2450 \* \* \* \* \* PAGE \\* MERGEFORMAT 8 \* .\* .\* .\* ) \* ( )\* ( )\* ( )\* ( )\* ( ) -240 \* SEQ CHAPTER \ \ 1 State Court Administrative Office -240 Model Local Administrative Order 48 Friend of the Court Alternative Dispute Resolution\* insert_number_-_insert_title\* insert_number_-_insert_title (rev. 09/19) -240 [LOCAL COURT LETTERHEAD] -240 Administrative Order [year] - [number] FRIEND OF THE COURT ALTERNATIVE DISPUTE RESOLUTION Previous Friend of the Court Alternative Dispute Resolution Local Administrative Orders Rescinded by this Order: IT IS ORDERED: P ursuant to MCR 3.224, the following is adopted at the [insert local court number] as the Friend of the Court Alternative Dispute Resolution Plan: (A) -540 Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR) General Provisions The [insert local court number] has determined that Friend of the Court (FOC) ADR can assist parties and the court in resolving cus \* FILLIN X \\* MERGEFORMAT tody, parenting time, and support disputes . 1) -720 The [insert local court number] will use the following ADR practices: -630 ( X ) FOC domestic relations mediation as established in MCL 552.513 and MCR 3.224, ( ) f acil itative and information-gathering c onference as established in MCR 3.224, ( ) joint m eetings established in MCL 552.642a and MCR 3.224, ( ) t he [insert local court number] has establish ed the following additional FOC ADR processes [explain name and legal authority] . -720 2) -720 All FOC cases must be screened for domestic violence using SCAO s screening protocol before the ADR process begins. If domestic violence is identified or suspected, the ADR process may not continue unless the protected party submits a wri tten consent and the FOC takes additional precautions to ensure the safety of the protected party and court staff. Throughout the ADR process, the mediator or facilitator must make reasonable efforts to screen for the presence of coerci on or violence that would make ADR physically or emotionally unsafe for any participant or that would impede achieving a voluntary and safe resolution of issues. -720 3) -720 I n accordance with MCL 552.505a , the FOC may provide ADR services for all open FOC cases that qualify for FOC ADR services . -720 4) -720 A party may object to FOC ADR under MCR 3.224(E) . An objection must be based on one or more of the factors listed in MCR 3. 224 (D)(2) : -720 -360 (a) child abuse or neglect; (b) domestic abuse, unless the protected party submits a written consent and the friend of the court takes additional precautions to ensure the safety of the protected party and court staff; (c) inability of one or both parties to negotiate for themselves at the ADR, unless attorneys for both parties will be present at the ADR session; (d) reason to believe that one or both parties health or safety would be endangered by ADR; or (e) for other good cause shown. -720 -720 and must allege facts in support of the objection. Timely objections must be made in accordance with MCR 3.224(E). 5) -720 Parties who are, or have been, subject to a personal protection order or other protective order or who are involved in a past or present chil d abuse and neglect proceeding may not be referred to FOC ADR without a hearing to determine whether FOC ADR is appropriate. The court may order ADR if a protected party requests it without holding a hearing. -720 6) -720 The FOC may exempt cases from ADR based on MCR 3.224(D)(2) . The FOC shall notify the court when it exempt s a case from FOC ADR. If the FOC exempts a case from ADR, a party may file a motion and schedule a hearing to request the court to order FOC ADR. -720 7) -720 Attorneys of record will be allowed to attend, and participate in, all FOC ADR processes, or elect not to attend upon mutual agreement with opposing counsel and their client. -720 8) -720 Participants in an ADR process may not record the ADR proceeding. -720 9) -720 The FOC shall provide a report with each FOC ADR proposed consent order containing sufficient information to allow the court to make an independent determination that the proposed order is in the child s best interest. -720 10) -720 When the parties do not resolve some or all of the issues in a facil itative and information-gathering conference or when the FOC submits a proposed order following a joint meeting, the FOC shall submit a report containing the parties agreed-upon and disputed facts and issues. -720 11) -720 Qualifications: FOC ADR providers ( ) have met the training and qualifications established by SCAO and approved by the c hief j udge ( ) and ( ) have been approved subject to conditions established by SCAO. -720 12) -720 Public Access to FOC ADR Plan: The FOC will ma ke the FOC ADR plan available : ( ) On the court s website -720 ( ) By way of pamphlet, handbook, or other publication ( ) Other (specify) _________________ (B) -540 Submitting Cases to ADR -720 ( ) On written stipulation of the parties, on motion of a party, or on the court s initiative, the court may order any contested ( ) prejudgment ( ) and ( ) postjudgment custody, parenting time, or support issue in a domestic relations case, including postjudgment matters to FOC mediation by written order. -720 -720 ( ) All ( ) prejudgment ( ) postjudgment custody, parenting time, and support issues are ordered to a facilitative and information-gathering conference. ( ) On written stipulation of the parties, on motion of a party, or on the court s own initiative, the court may order the parties to attend a facilitative and information-gathering conference. ( ) On written stipulation of the parties, on motion of a party, on the court s own initiative, the court may order the parties to attend a joint meeting postjudgment . ( ) The FOC may schedule a joint meeting with parties to a postjudgment custody, parenting time, and support dispute. -720 (C) -540 ADR Procedures 1) -720 FOC Domestic Relations Mediation FOC domestic relations mediation is a process in which a neutral third party facilitates confidential communication between parties to explore solutions to settle custody and parenting time or support issues for FOC cases. a. -720 Objection to Mediation: A party who is ordered to FOC domestic relation s mediation may file a written motion to remove the case from FOC med iation and a notice of hearing of the motion, and serve a copy on all parties or their attorneys of record within 14 days after receiving notice of the order. The motion must be set for hearing within 14 days after it is filed, unless the hearin g is adjourned by agreement of counsel or the court orders otherwise. A timely objection will be heard before the case is mediated by the FOC . -720 b. -720 FOC Domestic Relations Mediation Procedures: FOC domestic relations m ediation will be conducted by a mediator selected by the FOC. -720 i. -720 At the beginning of the mediation, the mediator wil l advise the parties and their attorneys, if applicable, of the following: a) -720 The purpose of mediation; b) -720 How the mediator will conduct mediation; c) Except as provided for in MCR 2.412(D)(8), statements made during the mediation process are confidential and cannot be used in court proceedings and cannot be recorded . -720 ii. -720 If the parties reach an agreement, the mediato r shall submit a proposed order and a report pursuant to MCR 3.224(I) within seven days. -720 iii. -720 If the parties do not reach an agreement within seven days of the completion of mediation, the mediator shall so advise the court stating only the date of completion of the process, who participated in the mediation, whether settlement was reached, and whether additional FOC ADR proceedings are contemplated. -720 iv. -720 With the exceptions provided for in MCR 2.412(D), communications during FOC domestic relations mediation process are confidential and cannot be used in court proceedings and cannot be recorded. 2) -360 ( ) Facilitative and Information-Gathering Conference [ Delete if not applicable, and delete this note. ] -720 A n FOC facilitative and information-gathering c onference is a process in which a facilitator assists the pa rties in reaching an agreement regarding custody, parenting time, and support . If the parties fail to reach an agreement, the facilitator may prepare a report and/or recommended order. a. -720 Object ions to a Facilitative and Information-Gathering Conference: -720 i. -720 To object to a FOC facilitative and information-gathering c onference, a party must include the objection within the pleading or post - judgment motion initiating the action, a responsive pleading or answer, or file the objection within 14 days of the date that th e notice is sent to the party. All objections must be filed with the court. -720 ii. -720 The objecting p arty must schedule the hearing and serve a copy of the objection and notice of hearing on all parties and/or attorneys of record. -720 iii. -720 If a party timely obj ects, the FOC shall not hold a facilitative and information-gathering c onference unless the court orders a conference after motion and hearing or the objecting party withdraws the objection . -720 b. -720 FOC Facilitative and Information-Gathering Conference Procedures: An FOC facilitative and information-gathering c onference shall use the following procedure s : i. -720 At the beginning of the conference, the facilitator will advise the parties and their attorneys, if applicable, of the following: -720 a) -720 T he purpose of the conference and how the facilitator will conduct the conference and submit an order or recommendation to the court under MCR 3.224( F) ; -720 b) -720 H ow information gathered during the conference will be used; -720 c) -720 T hat statements made during the conference are not confidential and can be used in other court proceedings, and shall not be recorded; and -720 d) -720 T hat the parties are expected to provide information as required by MCL 552.603 to the FOC and the consequences of not doing so. -720 ii. -720 If the partie s resolve all contested issues, the facilitator shall submit a report pursuant to MCR 3.224(I) ( ) and shall provide a proposed order to the court setting forth the parties agreements. -720 iii. -720 If the parties do not resolve all contested issues at the conference or the parties agree to resolve all or some contested issues but do not sign the proposed order, the facilitator shall submit a report pursuant to MCR 3.224(I). ( ) The facilitator shall also: -720 ( ) Prepare and forward a recommended order to the co urt within seven days from the date of the conference. The court may enter the recommended order if it approves the order and must serve it on all parties and attorneys of record within seven days from the date the court enters the order. Accompanying the order must be a notice that a party may object to the order by filing a written objection with the court within 21 days after the date of service, and by scheduling a hearing on the objection. If there is a timely objection, the court will hold a hearing within 21 days after the objection is filed. If a party objects, the order remains in effect pending a hearing on a party s objection unless the court orders otherwise. -720 -720 ( ) Prepare and serve a recommended o rder on the parties within seven days from the date of the conference along with a notice that the recommended order will be presented to the court for entry unless a party objects by filing a written objection within 21 days after the date of service, and by scheduling a hearing on th e objection. If neither party files a timely objection, the court may enter the order if it approves. -720 -720 ( ) Submit a recommendation to the court for further action the court might take to help the parties resolve the remaining contested issues in the case, or alert the court there are contested issues that might require the court s immediate attention. -720 ( ) [O nly use if the court chooses one of the first two options above . ] A party may consent to entry of a recommended order by signing a copy of the order at the time of the conference or after receiving the recommended order. A party who consents to entry of the order waives the right to object to the order and must file a motion to set the order aside once it enters. iv. -720 Except for communications made during do mestic violence screening , communications made during a facilitative and information-gathering conference are not confiden tial and may be used in court proceedings. 3) -360 ( ) Joint Meetings [ Delete if not applicable , and delete this note. ] -720 Joint meeting s are a process in which a person discusses proposed solutions with the parties to a custody or parenting time complaint or an objection to an FOC support recommendation. a. -720 Objection to a Joint Meeting: i. -720 To object to a joint meeting, the party must file a written objection with the FOC and provide a copy to all parties and their attorneys of record before the time scheduled for the joint meeting. ii. -720 If a party files an objection, the FOC shall not hold a joint meeting unless the court orders a joint meeting foll owing a hearing on motion of a party or the objecting party withdraws the objection. b. -720 Joint Meeting Procedures: Joint meetings shall be conducted as follows: i. -720 At the beginning of a joint meetin g, the person conducting the meeting shall do the following: a) -720 A dvise the parties that state ments made during the joint meeting are not confidential an d can be used in other court proceedings; -720 b) -720 A dvise the parties that the purpose of the meeting is for the parties to reach an accommodation and how the pe rson will conduct the meeting; and -720 c) -720 Advise the parties that the person may recommend an order to the court to resolve the dispute , an d explain to the parties the in formation provided for in MCR 3.224(H)(1)(d)-(e). ii. -720 At the conclusion of a joint me eting, the person conducting the meeting shall either terminate the meeting without further action or do one of the following within seven days : a) -720 If the parties reach an accommodation, record the accommodation in writing and provide a copy to the parties and attorneys of record. If the accommodation modifies an order, the person must submit a proposed order and a report pursuant to MCR 3.224(I) to the court. If the court approves the order, the court shall enter it; or -720 b) -720 Submit an order to the court stating the person s recommendation for resolving the dispute with a report pursuant to MCR 3.224(I) . The parties may consent by signing the recommended order and waiving the objection period in accordance with MCR 3.224(H)(1)(e)(iii). If the court approves the order, the court shall enter it. -720 iii. -720 If the person conducting the joint meeting submi ts a recommended order to the court, t he FOC must serve the parties and attorneys of record with a copy of the ord er and a notice that provides the following information: a) -720 That the court may enter the recommended order resolving the dispute unless a party objects to the order in writing w ithin 21 days after the notice is sent; -720 b) -720 The objection must be sent to [state where to file the objection] ; c) -720 That a party may waive the 21-day objection period by returning a signed copy of the recommended order; -720 d) -720 I f a party files a written objection within the 21-day limit, the FOC shall set a court hearing before a judge or referee to resolve the dispute. If a party fails to file a written objection within the 21-day limit, the FOC shall submit the proposed order to the court for entry if the court approves it; -720 e) -720 E xcept for communications made during domestic violence screening, communications made during a joint meeting are not confidential and may be used in other court proceedings and can not be recorded. Dated: _______________ Chief judge s ignature: _________________________________ \* The purpose of this order is to establish an Alternative Contempt Track docket as provided in MCL 552.635a upon approval by the State Court Administrative Office. Circuit Court establishes the Alternative Contempt Track Docket according to the following terms: 1. -360 Assignment Click or tap here to enter text. Circuit/District/Probate Judge Click or tap here to enter text. is assigned to the ACT. Click or tap here to enter text. Circuit Referee is assigned to the ACT 2. -360 Eligibility To be eligible for the ACT, an individual must meet the following conditions: \'3f [ State the type of issue you are trying to address ] \'3f [ State any arrearage requirements ] -360 \'3f The individual must sign an agreement pursuant to paragraph 4 to participate in a service plan \'3f [State any o ther eligibility requirements ] \'3f [State what s creening or assessment will be done to determine eligibility] 3. -360 Other Eligibility \'3f The individual would be eligible to be set for a contempt hearing \'3f The individual has been found in contempt -360 \'3f The individual has been referred to the ACT by a friend of the court (FOC) case worker 4. -360 Intake Process Upon entering the program, the FOC/ court will follow this process: \'3f S et an initial meeting to explain the program \'3f H old a service plan structuring conference \'3f The FOC will p ropose a service plan to court for adoption \'3f The FOC will d evelop a service plan with the individual to propose to the court \'3f Have the p arent sign an agreement \'3f The Court will enter an order approving the parent s admission to the program 5. -360 Service Plan The service plan will include all of the following: \'3f The parent s goals \'3f The issues to be addressed \'3f The services that will be provided \'3f Milestones including support payments \'3f Timelines to achieve milestones \'3f Reporting requirements -360 \'3f The parent s acknowledgment that failure to attend any hearing could result in a warrant for the parent s arrest -360 \'3f Conditions that will lead to sanctions or dismissal and notice that sanctions, including incarceration, can be imposed for failure to comply with the service plan 6. -360 Statistical Reporting Requirements [ Enter any statistical information the FOC/Court will collect and analyze] 7. -360 Review Hearings -360 a. The court will hold review hearings to monitor progress toward fulfilling the case service plan: \'3f Early Review Hearings every Click or tap here to enter text. days/weeks/months -360 \'3f Maintenance Review Hearings every Click or tap here to enter text. days/weeks/months \'3f Permanency/Discharge Hearings every ____ days/weeks/months b. The hearings will have the following format: \'3f Review of the case service p lan \'3f Review milestones \'3f Review accomplishments towards milestones \'3f Review issues encountered \'3f Review next steps/modify plan as necessary -360 \'3f Reviewing the payer s d ue diligence in following the plan to further the payer s ability to pay support in accordance with MCL 552 .631 et. seq. \'3f Failure to appear at a hearing is considered a failure to appear pursuant to MCL 5 52.63 1 and the court may enter a warrant to have the parent brought into court to participate in the hearing c. The court will apply rewards/sanctions as necessary -360 \'3f Rewards include: \'3f Sanctions include: \'3f Incarceration for up to 10 days as necessary to address the seriousness of a failure to comply with the service plan \'3f Incarceration for up to 45 days with purge conditions pursuant to MCL 5 5 2 .63 1 et. seq . sufficient to bring the payer in compliance with the plan -360 \'3f Community service \'3f Tether \'3f Dismissal from the ACT d. At the conclusion of each hearing the court will enter an order: \'3f D etermining whether the payer is following the plan \'3f S tating the next steps each participant must take in furtherance of the plan \'3f S etting forth whether the payer has been rewarded or a sanction has been imposed \'3f S tating the date of the next hearing -360 \'3f P roviding notice of any additional actions the court may take such as modifying an order \'3f Stating that the payer must attend and that if the payer does not appear, the court may issue a warrant to have the payer brought into court. 8. -360 Support -360 \'3f Upon entering the ACT program, the court will provide notice to the support payee that support is being reviewed and can be modified effective the date of the first hearing. \'3f At any hearing, the court may stay the support order . The stay operates to: \'3f Pause the support charge upon notice and an opportunity for the payee to participate in the hearing -360 \'3f Pause the collection of support but continue the charge \'3f Other -360 \'3f The court may enter a temporary support order for all or part of the time the parent is in the ACT program upon notice and an opportunity for the payee to participate in the hearing . -360 \'3f The court upon notice and an opportunity for a hearing may modify support : -360 \'3f The court shall enter a final support order upon completion or termination of the ACT program. Either party may object to a proposed final support order. If an objection is made, the court will hold a separate hearing on the matter of entry of a final sup port order. \'3f The court may discharge arrears owed to the state with the state's approval and may also discharge arrears owed to a payee with the payee's consent upon successful completion of the ACT. 9. -360 Graduation and Dismissal The participant will complete the ACT program once the court determines the participant has substantially completed the plan. The participant will be dismissed from the ACT program upon the court s determination that the participant is not making progress toward completing the plan and after sanctions fail to obtain compliance. ________________________ ________ Chief Judge Signature Date \*  Report  By Adam  Ludovicz,
 08/09/25  09:51
Case  #NS2505078
Typed  By Adam  Ludovicz
8/9/25
LOCATION:
E NORTON/GETTY
HARASSMENT
I was dispatched  to tx caller  regarding  an incident  this morning  with their  daughter's  baby's  father. I spoke  to LORI
WATSON  via tx.
LORI advised  that her daughter,  EMILY  WATSON,  has a child with ANDREW  PIGORS.  EMILY  has a PPO against
ANDREW.  LORI stated  that ANDREW  is going through  some sort of mental  crisis lately and has been acting strange.
ANDREW  is living out of his car and was kicked  out of the trai!er  park in North Muskegon  that he was living in. He has
been seen driving  by LORI'S  house  at night and seen by others  acting  strange  out in public.
LORI said this morning  she was in her car and pulled out onto Getty  St. She said ANDREW  was driving  the opposite
way and made an abrupt  u-turn in the road and almost  caused  an accident.  ANDREW  and the motorist  he almost  hit
then drove off and went  down Wayne  St.
LORI and her husband  went  to check  on the incident  because  they are aware  of ANDREW'S  current  state. ANDREW
was verbal  with LORI and made comments  about  what  it feels like to be followed.  ANDREW  has been having paranoid
thoughts  where  he thinks  lots of people  are following  him trying  to get him.
LORI said she then drove  away and was unsure  where  ANDREW  went. LORI will be contacting  court next week  to try
an obtain a PPO since his behavior  makes her nervous  and his actions  near her and her residence.
DISPOSITION:
LORI had an interaction  with ANDREW  this morning  that she wanted  documented.  She will try to get a PPO for herself
against  ANDREW.  His whereabouts  are unknown  at this time but he drives  a gray Honda  Accord.  Report  Only.
LUDOVICZ  47416

Typed  By Adam  Ludovicz
8/9/25
LOCATION:
E NORTON/GETTY
HARASSMENT
I was dispatched  to tx caller  regarding  an incident  this morning  with their  daughter's  baby's  father. I spoke  to LORI
WATSON  via tx.
LORI advised  that her daughter,  EMILY  WATSON,  has a child with ANDREW  PIGORS.  EMILY  has a PPO against
ANDREW.  LORI stated  that ANDREW  is going through  some sort of mental  crisis lately and has been acting strange.
ANDREW  is living out of his car and was kicked  out of the trai!er  park in North Muskegon  that he was living in. He has
been seen driving  by LORI'S  house  at night and seen by others  acting  strange  out in public.
LORI said this morning  she was in her car and pulled out onto Getty  St. She said ANDREW  was driving  the opposite
way and made an abrupt  u-turn in the road and almost  caused  an accident.  ANDREW  and the motorist  he almost  hit
then drove off and went  down Wayne  St.
LORI and her husband  went  to check  on the incident  because  they are aware  of ANDREW'S  current  state. ANDREW
was verbal  with LORI and made comments  about  what  it feels like to be followed.  ANDREW  has been having paranoid
thoughts  where  he thinks  lots of people  are following  him trying  to get him.
LORI said she then drove  away and was unsure  where  ANDREW  went. LORI will be contacting  court next week  to try
an obtain a PPO since his behavior  makes her nervous  and his actions  near her and her residence.
DISPOSITION:
LORI had an interaction  with ANDREW  this morning  that she wanted  documented.  She will try to get a PPO for herself
against  ANDREW.  His whereabouts  are unknown  at this time but he drives  a gray Honda  Accord.  Report  Only.
LUDOVICZ  47416

Original
 Report  By Ella  Randall,  08/07/25  22:14
Case  #NS2505044
Typed  By Ella Randall
Date: 8/7/25
Location:  1143 E Norton Ave
I was dispatched  to the listed location  in reference  to a harassment  report. The caller, Albert  Watson,  reported  his
daughters  ex boyfriend  called him saying  the FBI was after their family.
I arrived  on scene  and made contact  with Albert  and his daughter,  Emily Watkins.  Emily started  off by advising  she and
her child's father, Andrew  Pigors, have been broken  up for 2 years. She said she has a PPO in place and only has
contact  with Andrew  to exchange  their  son. The PPO does not include  her Family. Albert  said that  today  he received  a
call from an unknown  number  and it was Andrew  saying  the FBI was after Albert's  family.  Albert  said his wife started
recording  the call shortly  after they realized  it was Andrew.  Albert  said on the call, Andrew  said there were people
following  Albert  and 
Emily and "they"  were  tracking  their  cars. Albert  played me a short snipit  or the recording.  I
immediately  heard Andrew  is noticeably  out of breathe.  Albert can be heard asking why Andrew  is out of breath and
Andrew  tells him he is being chased  but does not specify  by who. I asked Emily if she knows  of any mental health
problems  Andrew  has and she said there is a history  or drug use. Emily also advised  she believes  that Andrew  is
recently  homeless.
Albert  said they want  this incident  documented  so Emily can go tomorrow  to get an Ex Parte order  for full custody  of her
son. Emily said she is supposed  to exchange  her son with Andrew  tomorrow  and she does not feel comfortable  with
Andrew  caring for the child. I supplied  Albert  and Emily with a report  number  and cleared.  The recording  of the phone
call is attached  to this report.
Healthwest  referral  completed  for Andrew.
Randall  47437

 Report  By Ella  Randall,  08/07/25  22:14
Case  #NS2505044
Typed  By Ella Randall
Date: 8/7/25
Location:  1143 E Norton Ave
I was dispatched  to the listed location  in reference  to a harassment  report. The caller, Albert  Watson,  reported  his
daughters  ex boyfriend  called him saying  the FBI was after their family.
I arrived  on scene  and made contact  with Albert  and his daughter,  Emily Watkins.  Emily started  off by advising  she and
her child's father, Andrew  Pigors, have been broken  up for 2 years. She said she has a PPO in place and only has
contact  with Andrew  to exchange  their  son. The PPO does not include  her Family. Albert  said that  today  he received  a
call from an unknown  number  and it was Andrew  saying  the FBI was after Albert's  family.  Albert  said his wife started
recording  the call shortly  after they realized  it was Andrew.  Albert  said on the call, Andrew  said there were people
following  Albert  and 
Emily and "they"  were  tracking  their  cars. Albert  played me a short snipit  or the recording.  I
immediately  heard Andrew  is noticeably  out of breathe.  Albert can be heard asking why Andrew  is out of breath and
Andrew  tells him he is being chased  but does not specify  by who. I asked Emily if she knows  of any mental health
problems  Andrew  has and she said there is a history  or drug use. Emily also advised  she believes  that Andrew  is
recently  homeless.
Albert  said they want  this incident  documented  so Emily can go tomorrow  to get an Ex Parte order  for full custody  of her
son. Emily said she is supposed  to exchange  her son with Andrew  tomorrow  and she does not feel comfortable  with
Andrew  caring for the child. I supplied  Albert  and Emily with a report  number  and cleared.  The recording  of the phone
call is attached  to this report.
Healthwest  referral  completed  for Andrew.
Randall  47437

Case  #NS2500783
Typed  By Michael  Lowry
Date: 2/7/25
Location:  2160 Garland  Dr
I was dispatched  to TX Andrew  Pigors regarding  an other  investigation.
Speaking  with Andrevv  on TX, he advised  that he picked up his son, Lincoln Watson,  from Lincoln's  mother,  Emily
Watson,  today  and noticed  a bruise  and small cut on Lincoln's  head. Andrew  continued  by saying  that since July, when
their  custody  agreement  began, he has frequently  been noticing  bruises  on Lincoln when he picks him up from Emily
on the weekends.  Andrew  said that he never receives  and explanation  for the bruises  from Emily when he asks.
Andrew  said that Lincoln  was acting normal and did not appear  to be in distress.  Andrew  advised  that he had never
asked Lincoln  about  the bruises  and that Lincoln had never  said anything  about  them. Andrew  provided  a picture  of
Lincoln showing  a small  bruise  and a couple  of scratches  on his forehead.  I attached  the photo to this report.
Andrew  said he hoped Emily would not intentionally  hurt Lincoln  but had no way of knowing  if she was. Andrew
advised  that he did not intend to get Emily in trouble  but wanted  a report  made for court purposes.
I then went to the listed address  to contact  Emily. I spoke  with a resident  who advised  that Emily  was not currently
home but provided  a contact  phone  number  for her. While at the listed address,  I could see the house  through  the
windows,  and it appeared  to be neat and orderly,  with a large dog in the house.
I then spoke  with Emily  via TX. Emily explained  that bruise  and scratches  Andrew  was referencing  came  from Lincoln
playing  with a toy tractor.  Emily continued  by saying  that she was in the kitchen  while Lincoln  played  in the living room.
She heard Lincoln  cry, then went  to him and saw the scratches  on his head. Emily said that she has another  son in the
house and a large dog, and the previous  bruises  Andrew  mentioned  had come  from Lincoln  just playing  with the dog
and the other child. Emily  said that none of Lincoln's  claimed  bruises  have been given to him intentionally  by her or
anyone  else. Emily advised  that she had previously  answered  Andrew's  questions  regarding  Lincoln.
At this time I have no evidence  to support  any child abuse  taking place.
Closed

and the other child. Emily  said that none of Lincoln's  claimed  bruises  have been given to him intentionally  by her or
anyone  else. Emily advised  that she had previously  answered  Andrew's  questions  regarding  Lincoln.
At this time I have no evidence  to support  any child abuse  taking place.
Closed

Off Narrative
Case  #NSPD-2024-02086
Typed  By Ethan  Vanderlaan
1853 04/02/2024
2160 GARLAND
l was dispatched  to the above  location  for a check  wellbeing.  Andrew  Pigors  was request  a check on his 1.5 yr old son
who lives with his mother  Emily Watson.  Emily has a no contact  order against  Andrew  so he cannot  check  for himselt.
He said that he sent a process  server  to her house  to serve papers  and she did not answer  the door even though  her
car was there. He had no reason  to believe  the child was in any danger.
I made contact  with Emily at the above  location  and she showed  me to Lincoln.  He appeared  to be in fine health.
I notified  Andrew  of my findings  and he was satisfied.  I intormed  Andrew  that, while his reasoning  is understandable
his call does not constitute  an emergency  and he should  avoid making  similar  calls in the future  as it could be
interpreted  as misuse  of 911  He advised  that he understood.
No further  action taken.
VANDERLAAN

Off  Narrative
Case  #NSPD-2024-02001
Typed  By David  Harwood
3-29-2024
I was dispatched
 to an unwanted  person  at 1143  E Norton.  The  caller  advised  her daughteras  ex-boyfriend
 was  outside
refusing  to leave  and wanted  to see his son. While  enroute  dispatch  received  a call from  the male  half  who advised  he
would  be waiting  in the driveway.
I arrived  on scene  and made  contact  with Andrew  Pigors  who advised  he was  just  there  to see his son. Andrew  advised
he had dropped  his son off on Tuesday  to Lori Watson  who is his babysitter  and also his ex girlfriendos  mom.  He has
not seen  his son since.  Andrew  advised  he did not want  any issues  and would  leave  if he had to.
I made  contact  with Lori Watson  who  was  watching  Lincoln  Watson.  Lori advised  there  was no custody  agreement
 but
her daughter  Emily  was  in the process  of seeking  full custody  of Lincoln.  Lori advised  Emily  had a signed  Affidavit  of
Parentage.
 The  affidavit  was signed  by both Emily  and Andrew.  In the affidavit  it states  the mother  of the child has
initial  custody  of the child  and until determined
 otherwise  by the court.  Andrew  was  shown  this affidavit  and advised  to
contact  the Friend  of Court  in Muskegon  and pursue  a custody  agreement.  He advised  he would  do so.
All parties  were  advised  to contact  the Triend of court  and seek  a court  determined
 custody  agreement.
 Andrew  left the
residence  and advised  he would  not be back.  I cleared  with no further  actions.
Harwood  47412

 Report  By Matthew
 Brouwer,
 03/26/24  19:30
Off Narrative
Case  #NSPD-2024-01944
Typed  By Matthew  Brouwer
03/26/2024
1143 E Norton AV
I was dispatched  to the listed location  on report  of a custody  interference.  The caller,  Aridrevv Pigor advised  that he
dropped  off his son, Lincoln  Watson,  off at Emma  Watson"s  mother,  Lori Watsonas home. Andrew  adised  he was later
told that he could no longer  see his son. Andrew  was requesting  a TX.
Upon calling Andrew,  he stated  that he is unable  to directly  contact  the mother  of his son, Emma  Watson,  as she has a
PPO against  him. Because  of this, Andrew  uses Emma  s mother  Lori as a go-between  for custody  arrangements.
Andrew  stated that earlier  in the day on 3/26, he dropped  their son Lincoln  off at Lorias home on E Norton,  as he
usually  does. A short  time after dropping  Lincoln  off, Andrew  stated  he received  a text from Lori stating  that he could
not longer  see Lincoln  and would be trespassed  if he returned  to the E Norton address.  Andrew  stated  that he has
started  the process  to create  a formal  custody  agreement  through  Friend of the Court, but it has not yet been
approved.  l advised  Andrew  that this incident  would  be documented,  but no actions  could be taken to force Lori to allow
Andrew  to see his son.
Andrew  then requested  that I perform  a check  well-being  on Lincoln at Emmaas address  at 21 60 Garland,  as he was
worried  Emma  is not taking adequate  care of him.
03/26/2024
2160 Garland  St
Upon arrival, I made contact  with Emma, who willingly  allowed  me to enter  the residence.  Upon entering  the residence,
I found  the home to be tidy and a safe environment  for children.  Emma  then showed  me that Lincoln  was sleeping  in a
crib in a rear bedroom  of the home. Emma  picked  up Lincoln,  he appeared  to be healthy.  Emma  advised  that she is
declining  to let Lincoln  see Andrew  as she received  word  that his home  is not safe for children.

Information:
Albert  Watson  called 91 'l after serving  a PPO on Andrew  Pigors at the listed location.  Andrew  resides  at the home with
Watsonas  daughter,  Emily  Watson.  Upon arriving I made contact  with Both Albert  and Andrew.  Andrew  was in a
possession  of a copy of the PPO he had been served  by Albert. Andrew  collected  some  clothing  and toiletries  from the
home. He left without  incident.

Dispatched  entered  the PPO in LEIN as served  at my request.
Disposition:
No further  police assistance  was needed.

 Report  By Adam  Ludovicz,
 08/09/25  09:51
Case  #NS2505078
Typed  By Adam  Ludovicz
8/9/25
LOCATION:
E NORTON/GETTY
HARASSMENT
I was dispatched  to tx caller  regarding  an incident  this morning  with their  daughter's  baby's  father. I spoke  to LORI
WATSON  via tx.
LORI advised  that her daughter,  EMILY  WATSON,  has a child with ANDREW  PIGORS.  EMILY  has a PPO against
ANDREW.  LORI stated  that ANDREW  is going through  some sort of mental  crisis lately and has been acting strange.
ANDREW  is living out of his car and was kicked  out of the trai!er  park in North Muskegon  that he was living in. He has
been seen driving  by LORI'S  house  at night and seen by others  acting  strange  out in public.
LORI said this morning  she was in her car and pulled out onto Getty  St. She said ANDREW  was driving  the opposite
way and made an abrupt  u-turn in the road and almost  caused  an accident.  ANDREW  and the motorist  he almost  hit
then drove off and went  down Wayne  St.
LORI and her husband  went  to check  on the incident  because  they are aware  of ANDREW'S  current  state. ANDREW
was verbal  with LORI and made comments  about  what  it feels like to be followed.  ANDREW  has been having paranoid
thoughts  where  he thinks  lots of people  are following  him trying  to get him.
LORI said she then drove  away and was unsure  where  ANDREW  went. LORI will be contacting  court next week  to try
an obtain a PPO since his behavior  makes her nervous  and his actions  near her and her residence.
DISPOSITION:
LORI had an interaction  with ANDREW  this morning  that she wanted  documented.  She will try to get a PPO for herself
against  ANDREW.  His whereabouts  are unknown  at this time but he drives  a gray Honda  Accord.  Report  Only.
LUDOVICZ  47416

 Report  By Adam  Ludovicz,
 08/09/25  09:51
Case  #NS2505078
Typed  By Adam  Ludovicz
8/9/25
LOCATION:
E NORTON/GETTY
HARASSMENT
I was dispatched  to tx caller  regarding  an incident  this morning  with their  daughter's  baby's  father. I spoke  to LORI
WATSON  via tx.
LORI advised  that her daughter,  EMILY  WATSON,  has a child with ANDREW  PIGORS.  EMILY  has a PPO against
ANDREW.  LORI stated  that ANDREW  is going through  some sort of mental  crisis lately and has been acting strange.
ANDREW  is living out of his car and was kicked  out of the trai!er  park in North Muskegon  that he was living in. He has
been seen driving  by LORI'S  house  at night and seen by others  acting  strange  out in public.
LORI said this morning  she was in her car and pulled out onto Getty  St. She said ANDREW  was driving  the opposite
way and made an abrupt  u-turn in the road and almost  caused  an accident.  ANDREW  and the motorist  he almost  hit
then drove off and went  down Wayne  St.
LORI and her husband  went  to check  on the incident  because  they are aware  of ANDREW'S  current  state. ANDREW
was verbal  with LORI and made comments  about  what  it feels like to be followed.  ANDREW  has been having paranoid
thoughts  where  he thinks  lots of people  are following  him trying  to get him.
LORI said she then drove  away and was unsure  where  ANDREW  went. LORI will be contacting  court next week  to try
an obtain a PPO since his behavior  makes her nervous  and his actions  near her and her residence.
DISPOSITION:
LORI had an interaction  with ANDREW  this morning  that she wanted  documented.  She will try to get a PPO for herself
against  ANDREW.  His whereabouts  are unknown  at this time but he drives  a gray Honda  Accord.  Report  Only.

 Report  By Ella  Randall,  08/07/25  22:14
Case  #NS2505044
Typed  By Ella Randall
Date: 8/7/25
Location:  1143 E Norton Ave
I was dispatched  to the listed location  in reference  to a harassment  report. The caller, Albert  Watson,  reported  his
daughters  ex boyfriend  called him saying  the FBI was after their family.
I arrived  on scene  and made contact  with Albert  and his daughter,  Emily Watkins.  Emily started  off by advising  she and
her child's father, Andrew  Pigors, have been broken  up for 2 years. She said she has a PPO in place and only has
contact  with Andrew  to exchange  their  son. The PPO does not include  her Family. Albert  said that  today  he received  a
call from an unknown  number  and it was Andrew  saying  the FBI was after Albert's  family.  Albert  said his wife started
recording  the call shortly  after they realized  it was Andrew.  Albert  said on the call, Andrew  said there were people
following  Albert  and 
Emily and "they"  were  tracking  their  cars. Albert  played me a short snipit  or the recording.  I
immediately  heard Andrew  is noticeably  out of breathe.  Albert can be heard asking why Andrew  is out of breath and
Andrew  tells him he is being chased  but does not specify  by who. I asked Emily if she knows  of any mental health
problems  Andrew  has and she said there is a history  or drug use. Emily also advised  she believes  that Andrew  is
recently  homeless.
Albert  said they want  this incident  documented  so Emily can go tomorrow  to get an Ex Parte order  for full custody  of her
son. Emily said she is supposed  to exchange  her son with Andrew  tomorrow  and she does not feel comfortable  with
Andrew  caring for the child. I supplied  Albert  and Emily with a report  number  and cleared.  The recording  of the phone
call is attached  to this report.
Healthwest  referral  completed  for Andrew.
Randall  47437

 Report  By Ella  Randall,  08/07/25  22:14
Case  #NS2505044
Typed  By Ella Randall
Date: 8/7/25
Location:  1143 E Norton Ave
I was dispatched  to the listed location  in reference  to a harassment  report. The caller, Albert  Watson,  reported  his
daughters  ex boyfriend  called him saying  the FBI was after their family.
I arrived  on scene  and made contact  with Albert  and his daughter,  Emily Watkins.  Emily started  off by advising  she and
her child's father, Andrew  Pigors, have been broken  up for 2 years. She said she has a PPO in place and only has
contact  with Andrew  to exchange  their  son. The PPO does not include  her Family. Albert  said that  today  he received  a
call from an unknown  number  and it was Andrew  saying  the FBI was after Albert's  family.  Albert  said his wife started
recording  the call shortly  after they realized  it was Andrew.  Albert  said on the call, Andrew  said there were people
following  Albert  and 
Emily and "they"  were  tracking  their  cars. Albert  played me a short snipit  or the recording.  I
immediately  heard Andrew  is noticeably  out of breathe.  Albert can be heard asking why Andrew  is out of breath and
Andrew  tells him he is being chased  but does not specify  by who. I asked Emily if she knows  of any mental health
problems  Andrew  has and she said there is a history  or drug use. Emily also advised  she believes  that Andrew  is
recently  homeless.
Albert  said they want  this incident  documented  so Emily can go tomorrow  to get an Ex Parte order  for full custody  of her
son. Emily said she is supposed  to exchange  her son with Andrew  tomorrow  and she does not feel comfortable  with
Andrew  caring for the child. I supplied  Albert  and Emily with a report  number  and cleared.  The recording  of the phone
call is attached  to this report.
Healthwest  referral  completed  for Andrew.

 Report
 By  Michael
 Lowry,
 02/07/25

Case  #NS2500783
Typed  By Michael  Lowry
Date: 2/7/25
Location:  2160 Garland  Dr
I was dispatched  to TX Andrew  Pigors regarding  an other  investigation.
Speaking  with Andrevv  on TX, he advised  that he picked up his son, Lincoln Watson,  from Lincoln's  mother,  Emily
Watson,  today  and noticed  a bruise  and small cut on Lincoln's  head. Andrew  continued  by saying  that since July, when
their  custody  agreement  began, he has frequently  been noticing  bruises  on Lincoln when he picks him up from Emily
on the weekends.  Andrew  said that he never receives  and explanation  for the bruises  from Emily when he asks.
Andrew  said that Lincoln  was acting normal and did not appear  to be in distress.  Andrew  advised  that he had never
asked Lincoln  about  the bruises  and that Lincoln had never  said anything  about  them. Andrew  provided  a picture  of
Lincoln showing  a small  bruise  and a couple  of scratches  on his forehead.  I attached  the photo to this report.
Andrew  said he hoped Emily would not intentionally  hurt Lincoln  but had no way of knowing  if she was. Andrew
advised  that he did not intend to get Emily in trouble  but wanted  a report  made for court purposes.
I then went to the listed address  to contact  Emily. I spoke  with a resident  who advised  that Emily  was not currently
home but provided  a contact  phone  number  for her. While at the listed address,  I could see the house  through  the
windows,  and it appeared  to be neat and orderly,  with a large dog in the house.
I then spoke  with Emily  via TX. Emily explained  that bruise  and scratches  Andrew  was referencing  came  from Lincoln
playing  with a toy tractor.  Emily continued  by saying  that she was in the kitchen  while Lincoln  played  in the living room.
She heard Lincoln  cry, then went  to him and saw the scratches  on his head. Emily said that she has another  son in the
house and a large dog, and the previous  bruises  Andrew  mentioned  had come  from Lincoln  just playing  with the dog
and the other child. Emily  said that none of Lincoln's  claimed  bruises  have been given to him intentionally  by her or
anyone  else. Emily advised  that she had previously  answered  Andrew's  questions  regarding  Lincoln.
At this time I have no evidence  to support  any child abuse  taking place.
Closed

asked Lincoln  about  the bruises  and that Lincoln had never  said anything  about  them. Andrew  provided  a picture  of
Lincoln showing  a small  bruise  and a couple  of scratches  on his forehead.  I attached  the photo to this report.
Andrew  said he hoped Emily would not intentionally  hurt Lincoln  but had no way of knowing  if she was. Andrew
advised  that he did not intend to get Emily in trouble  but wanted  a report  made for court purposes.
I then went to the listed address  to contact  Emily. I spoke  with a resident  who advised  that Emily  was not currently
home but provided  a contact  phone  number  for her. While at the listed address,  I could see the house  through  the
windows,  and it appeared  to be neat and orderly,  with a large dog in the house.
I then spoke  with Emily  via TX. Emily explained  that bruise  and scratches  Andrew  was referencing  came  from Lincoln
playing  with a toy tractor.  Emily continued  by saying  that she was in the kitchen  while Lincoln  played  in the living room.
She heard Lincoln  cry, then went  to him and saw the scratches  on his head. Emily said that she has another  son in the
house and a large dog, and the previous  bruises  Andrew  mentioned  had come  from Lincoln  just playing  with the dog
and the other child. Emily  said that none of Lincoln's  claimed  bruises  have been given to him intentionally  by her or
anyone  else. Emily advised  that she had previously  answered  Andrew's  questions  regarding  Lincoln.
At this time I have no evidence  to support  any child abuse  taking place.
Closed

l was dispatched  to the above  location  for a check  wellbeing.  Andrew  Pigors  was request  a check on his 1.5 yr old son
who lives with his mother  Emily Watson.  Emily has a no contact  order against  Andrew  so he cannot  check  for himselt.
He said that he sent a process  server  to her house  to serve papers  and she did not answer  the door even though  her
car was there. He had no reason  to believe  the child was in any danger.
I made contact  with Emily at the above  location  and she showed  me to Lincoln.  He appeared  to be in fine health.
I notified  Andrew  of my findings  and he was satisfied.  I intormed  Andrew  that, while his reasoning  is understandable
his call does not constitute  an emergency  and he should  avoid making  similar  calls in the future  as it could be
interpreted  as misuse  of 911  He advised  that he understood.
No further  action taken.
VANDERLAAN

 to an unwanted  person  at 1143  E Norton.  The  caller  advised  her daughteras  ex-boyfriend
 was  outside
refusing  to leave  and wanted  to see his son. While  enroute  dispatch  received  a call from  the male  half  who advised  he
would  be waiting  in the driveway.
I arrived  on scene  and made  contact  with Andrew  Pigors  who advised  he was  just  there  to see his son. Andrew  advised
he had dropped  his son off on Tuesday  to Lori Watson  who is his babysitter  and also his ex girlfriendos  mom.  He has
not seen  his son since.  Andrew  advised  he did not want  any issues  and would  leave  if he had to.
I made  contact  with Lori Watson  who  was  watching  Lincoln  Watson.  Lori advised  there  was no custody  agreement
 but
her daughter  Emily  was  in the process  of seeking  full custody  of Lincoln.  Lori advised  Emily  had a signed  Affidavit  of
Parentage.
 The  affidavit  was signed  by both Emily  and Andrew.  In the affidavit  it states  the mother  of the child has
initial  custody  of the child  and until determined
 otherwise  by the court.  Andrew  was  shown  this affidavit  and advised  to
contact  the Friend  of Court  in Muskegon  and pursue  a custody  agreement.  He advised  he would  do so.
All parties  were  advised  to contact  the Triend of court  and seek  a court  determined
 custody  agreement.
 Andrew  left the
residence  and advised  he would  not be back.  I cleared  with no further  actions.

I was dispatched  to the listed location  on report  of a custody  interference.  The caller,  Aridrevv Pigor advised  that he
dropped  off his son, Lincoln  Watson,  off at Emma  Watson"s  mother,  Lori Watsonas home. Andrew  adised  he was later
told that he could no longer  see his son. Andrew  was requesting  a TX.
Upon calling Andrew,  he stated  that he is unable  to directly  contact  the mother  of his son, Emma  Watson,  as she has a
PPO against  him. Because  of this, Andrew  uses Emma  s mother  Lori as a go-between  for custody  arrangements.
Andrew  stated that earlier  in the day on 3/26, he dropped  their son Lincoln  off at Lorias home on E Norton,  as he
usually  does. A short  time after dropping  Lincoln  off, Andrew  stated  he received  a text from Lori stating  that he could
not longer  see Lincoln  and would be trespassed  if he returned  to the E Norton address.  Andrew  stated  that he has
started  the process  to create  a formal  custody  agreement  through  Friend of the Court, but it has not yet been
approved.  l advised  Andrew  that this incident  would  be documented,  but no actions  could be taken to force Lori to allow
Andrew  to see his son.
Andrew  then requested  that I perform  a check  well-being  on Lincoln at Emmaas address  at 21 60 Garland,  as he was
worried  Emma  is not taking adequate  care of him.
03/26/2024
2160 Garland  St
Upon arrival, I made contact  with Emma, who willingly  allowed  me to enter  the residence.  Upon entering  the residence,
I found  the home to be tidy and a safe environment  for children.  Emma  then showed  me that Lincoln  was sleeping  in a
crib in a rear bedroom  of the home. Emma  picked  up Lincoln,  he appeared  to be healthy.  Emma  advised  that she is
declining  to let Lincoln  see Andrew  as she received  word  that his home  is not safe for children.
